2606.05106.title: Arithmetic Pedagogy for Language Models
2606.05106.paper_summary: We investigate whether methods of human mathematics pedagogy can guide the training of language models toward arithmetic reasoning. Building on the GASING method -- an Indonesian pedagogy that solves basic arithmetic through a left-to-right procedure aligned with the causal order of token generation -- we operationalize each operation as a computational procedure whose execution trace is serialized into natural-language Chain-of-Thought (CoT) supervision. A small GPT-2 decoder (86M parameters) with a syllabic-agglutinative TOBA tokenizer for Indonesian is trained from scratch on this data using only a next-token prediction objective, without reinforcement learning or reward-based optimization. Monitoring training reveals three distinct learning phases, and mechanistic analyses -- attention-masking interventions on the CoT information graph, residual-stream probing, and logit-lens inspection -- show that the model first internalizes a procedural pathway and subsequently develops an associative, ``mental-arithmetic'' capacity that retrieves intermediate results without explicit step-by-step computation. The trained model reaches over 80% accuracy on held-out problems and attains competitive performance against substantially larger language models, indicating that targeted, pedagogically grounded training can yield strong and economical arithmetic capability at small scale.
2606.05106.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究人類數學教學方法是否可以指導語言模型的訓練以進行算術推理。基於GASING方法——這是一種印尼教學法，通過與標記生成的因果順序對齊的從左到右程序解決基本算術——我們將每個操作具體化為一個計算程序，其執行過程被序列化為自然語言的思考鏈（CoT）監督。使用印尼語的音節聚合TOBA分詞器從零開始訓練一個小型的GPT-2解碼器（86M參數），僅使用下一個標記預測目標，沒有強化學習或基於獎勵的優化。監控訓練顯示出三個不同的學習階段，機械分析——對CoT信息圖的注意力遮蔽干預、殘差流探測和邏輯透鏡檢查——顯示模型首先內化了一個程序路徑，隨後發展出一種聯想的“心算”能力，能夠在不明確逐步計算的情況下檢索中間結果。訓練後的模型在保留問題上達到超過80%的準確率，並在與大得多的語言模型競爭中表現出色，這表明有針對性的、以教學為基礎的訓練可以在小規模下產生強大而經濟的算術能力。
2606.05106.author: Andhika Bernard Lumbantobing et.al.
